semanticCore: Rising, emerging, cut skin, blood flow, or shining star
quranicRelevance: Rising moon/sun in Abraham's search (Bazighan - 'al-qamara bazighan', 'ash-shamsa bazighatan').

keyDerivations.meaning: To rise, emerge, shine, start to glow, or appear in the sky

figurativeOrRareUsages: فَلَمَّا رَأَى الشَّمْسَ بَازِغَةً قَالَ هَٰذَا رَبِّي هَٰذَا أَكْبَرُ (6:78): And when he saw the sun rising/emerging, he said: 'This is my Lord; this is greater' (Abraham's pedagogical stellar review).
discardedNoise: Bleeding a camel's veins by cutting skin (Bazgh).
summaryEn: The root represents celestial rising, tracking Abraham's progressive theological critique of the rising moon and sun to prove the transience of created bodies.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر طلوع الكواكب وبداية إشراق ضوئها بالسموات (بزوغ)، مصوراً حجة إبراهيم المتدرجة في بزوغ القمر ثم بزوغ الشمس لإبطال ربوبيتها.
rawText: إِبْتَزَغَ 8 see 7. انبزغ الرَّبِيعُ 7 , (S, and so in a copy of the K,) or 🡻ابتزغ, (so in other copies of the K and in the TA,) The first, or beginning, of the [season, or rain, or herbage, called] ربيع came. (S, K.) بَزَّغَ 2 see 1. بُزُوغٌ 1 [inf. n. of بَزَغَ] signifies The beginning to rise, or come forth: this is the primary meaning: mentioned by Zj. (TA.) === Hence, (TA,) بَزَغَ, said of a tush, or tusk, or canine tooth, (A,) or of the tush of a camel, (S, Msb, K,) [aor. ﹹ,] inf. n. بُزُوغٌ, (Msb,) It came forth; (S, Msb, K;) it clave the flesh, and came forth. (A.) === And hence, (A, TA,) بَزَغَتِ الشَّمْسُ, (JK, S, A, Msb, K,) aor. ﹹ, (TK,) inf. n. as above (JK, S, K) and بَزْغٌ, (K,) The sun began to rise; (JK, TA;) as though it clave the darkness with its light: (A, TA:) or rose, (S, Msb, K,) with spreading light: (TA:) or بُزُوغٌ has the meaning first explained above; the beginning to rise, or come forth. (K.) And in like manner one says, بَزَغَ القَمَرُ [The moon began to rise: or rose]. (A, TA.) ___ بَزَغَ, (S, Mgh, Msb, K,) aor. ﹹ, (Msb,) inf. n. بَزْغٌ, (JK, Msb,) He (a cupper, and a farrier,) scarified, (S, Msb, K,) and made the blood to flow: (Msb:) he (a farrier) scarified a beast (JK, Mgh, TA) in its أَشْعَر [or part next the hoof (in the TA, erroneously, شعر)], (JK,) with a مِبْزَع (JK, Mgh, TA) of iron; (JK;) as also 🡻بزّغ, inf. n. تَبْزِيغٌ: (JK, * TA:) Aboo- 'Adnán says that تَبْزِيغٌ and تَعْرِيبٌ signify the same, namely, the making a slight incision, or stab, such as does not reach the sinews, or tendons. (TA.) === And He made his blood to flow. (TA.) قَمَرٌ بَازِغٌ , (TA,) and شَمْسٌ بَازِغَةٌ, (Msb,) and نُجُومٌ بَوَازِغُ, (JK, A,) [A moon, and a sun, and stars,] beginning to rise: (JK, TA:) or rising. (Msb, TA.) مِبْزَغٌ A lancet (S, Mgh, K) of a cupper and of a farrier. (JK, Mgh, TA.)
